title: HiFlow: Training-free High-Resolution Image Generation with Flow-Aligned Guidance

repos.name: Bujiazi__HiFlow
repos.evidence: README explicitly states 'This is official code of HiFlow' and lists identical title/authors/NeurIPS 2025 venue and arXiv:2504.06232. Repo owner 'Bujiazi' is first author Jiazi Bu. Core method (flow-aligned guidance: initialization, direction, acceleration alignment) implemented in flux_pipeline_hiflow.py:flowmatch_step, matching paper Algorithm 1.

rationale: A single repo was cloned (Bujiazi__HiFlow) and it is unambiguously the official author implementation: the README declares it so, the repo owner matches the first author (Jiazi Bu / bujiazi), the paper title/venue/arXiv ID match exactly, and the core algorithm (flowmatch_step with init/direction/acceleration alignment) is faithfully present. The audit's findings are all written against this correct repo and concern missing evaluation infrastructure (no FID/IS/CLIP pipeline, no baselines, no prompt/reference data) and two low-severity config mismatches — none involve a wrong-repo error. No contamination by non-core repos exists.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified Bujiazi__HiFlow as the author repo. All findings (no-evaluation-pipeline, no-baseline-implementations, eval-prompt-set-missing, default-loads-lora-not-table1-config, default-cascade-skips-3k-stage) are written against the correct target repo. No findings need to be retracted or redirected.
recommended_action: No action required. The audit is correctly scoped to the one cloned repo, which is the paper's own official implementation.
